text:
in all those 2m I didn't see a single log update, I had no idea what the thing was doing -- I want it to tell me what its going on more proactively [...] Assistant: Now render the stage in the "current" region:
 [...] 1. **Live phase in the headline** ‚Äî the "current" box now narrates the actual stage (`starting` ‚Üí `extracting claims` ‚Üí `tagging authority` ‚Üí `routing to guides` ‚Üí `reconciling guides` ‚Üí `writing wiki` ‚Üí `rebuilding index`), driven by the session's own events, with a `¬∑ waiting on model` marker between an `llm.request` and its response. This is the piece that stays informative *through* a silent LLM call. [...]      - `CurrentSession` struct ‚Äî added `stage: String` and `waiting_on_model: bool`.
     - New helper `stage_label_for_event(event: &str) -> Option<&'static str>` mapping: capture.start‚Üí"extracting claims", capture.extract‚Üí"tagging authority", capture.authority_tagging/route_recall‚Üí"routing to guides", capture.route‚Üí"reconciling guides", wiki.*‚Üí"writing wiki", capture.agent_done‚Üí"rebuilding index".
     - SessionStart handler in `run_tui_mode` ‚Äî initialized new fields: `stage: "starting".to_string(), waiting_on_model: false`. [...]        ``` [...]    - **User feedback via advisor:** (1) Don't hardcode "triaging‚Ä¶" as initial label (triage is conditional) ‚Äî used "starting" instead. (2) The summary still misreports a q-interrupted session ‚Äî fixed via trailing-event drain after worker.join().
